description: Infrastructure MITM Investigation series. Each entry runs network probes and generates analysis.

files.note: Hop 8 discovery — MTR to all LLM providers, document the silent node at the Cogent transit boundary
TCP FIN injection on WebSocket, post-handshake timing, fallback to HTTPS
TCP/QUIC asymmetry — same client, same service, different transport, different outcome
TTL uniformity, latency anomaly, anycast hijacking mechanism, embedded CDN PoPs
Steelman and dismantle the benign explanation. Backbone proxy + BGP anycast + embedded CDN = full chain
Formal description of the backbone-proxy-to-embedded-CDN-PoP attack mechanism. Synthesis of Parts 1-5.
Longitudinal TTL monitoring — has the hop count or latency profile changed since initial probing? Cross-reference with process-net-monitor flow logs for historical TTL data.
Re-test QUIC vs TCP asymmetry. Has the middlebox adapted? Does HTTP/3 still bypass? Use bpfcc tcpretrans/tcpdrop to capture kernel-level TCP anomalies during the test.
Compare DNS resolution via system resolver vs DoH vs DoT. Check for DNS-layer manipulation. Cross-reference with process-net-monitor port 53 captures.
Compare TLS certificate fingerprints observed through Cogent vs from a non-Cogent path. Do the certs differ? Reference cert-pin TOFU baseline from ws-enforcement.
Reproduce and document I2P tunnel data corruption. Use bpftrace to instrument TCP checksum paths in kernel. Capture byte-level evidence of recalculation across proxy boundaries.
Fastly showed same TTL 55 / 9 hops as Cloudflare. Probe Fastly-fronted services specifically — is it the same convergent routing?

Deep analysis of process-net-monitor 62K+ flow records. Per-process connection patterns, deny-host frequency, TCP vs UDP ratio by destination, temporal patterns. Query openclaw and codex SQLite DBs for correlated session anomalies.
eBPF deep dive — bpftrace/bcc instrumentation of TCP stack: tcpretrans, tcpdrop, tcp_check_req_seq anomalies, sk_buff manipulation. Kernel-level evidence of proxy-induced TCP state corruption.
